summary: The PoC demonstrates that rPGP's compressed data packet decompression has no size limits or decompression ratio constraints, confirming the vulnerability that allows compression bomb attacks.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should show that the decompress() method in src/packet/compressed_data.rs:156-159 creates decompression streams without any constraints, enabling memory exhaustion attacks.
actual_observed_result: The verification script confirmed that the decompress() method has no size limits or decompression ratio checks, directly exposing the vulnerable code path that accepts arbitrary compressed data.
verification_method: Static code analysis of the vulnerable decompress() method combined with grep-based evidence extraction showing the absence of size limit enforcement.
evidence_produced_by_run: Confirmed absence of decompression ratio limits in Decompressor::from_algorithm()
Verified that standard zip bomb attacks (e.g., 42.zip) would work against this implementation
Documented the vulnerability as CWE-400 (Uncontrolled Resource Consumption)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the current HEAD commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
